perturbation.original_text: Unless specified otherwise, any payment to be made by Vericel under this Agreement shall be made within [***] from date of invoice. It is hereby agreed that the invoice with respect to any shipment will be issued upon the delivery date.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless specified otherwise, any payment to be made by Vericel under this Agreement shall be made within [***] from date of invoice. Notwithstanding the foregoing, invoices for shipments completed in the final calendar quarter shall be due 30 days from the end of the calendar quarter. It is hereby agreed that the invoice with respect to any shipment will be issued upon the delivery date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by adding a new payment term specifically for shipments in the final calendar quarter. This directly contradicts the original payment term of '[***] from date of invoice' for all payments, creating uncertainty about the actual due date for invoices in the final calendar quarter. This contradiction creates an in-text contradiction for invoices of shipments made in the final calendar quarter, since it is unclair when that will be, if [***] from invoice date or 30 days from the end of the calendar quarter.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless specified otherwise, any payment to be made by Vericel under this Agreement shall be made within [***] from date of invoice. Notwithstanding the foregoing, invoices for shipments completed in the final calendar quarter shall be due 30 days from the end of the calendar quarter. It is hereby agreed that the invoice with respect to any shipment will be issued upon the delivery date. The Parties' respective rights and responsibilities under Sections 5.6.5 and 5.6.6 of the License Agreement shall apply as such Section pertains to the Parties' performance under this Agreement, and are hereby incorporated by reference.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by specifying a different payment due date for shipments completed in the final calendar quarter. The original text states that all payments shall be made within [***] from the date of invoice. The change creates an exception, stating that invoices for shipments in the final calendar quarter are due 30 days from the end of that quarter. This creates a direct conflict regarding when payments are due for shipments made in the final calendar quarter, as it is unclear whether the original term of '[***] from date of invoice' or the new term of '30 days from the end of the calendar quarter' applies, resulting in an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that any payment due under this Agreement is not made when due, the amount due shall accrue interest beginning on the [***] following the date on which such payment was due, calculated at the [***] for the due date, or, if lower, the maximum rate permitted by law, calculated from the due date until paid in full. Each payment made after the due date shall be accompanied by all interest so accrued.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that any payment due under this Agreement is not made when due, the amount due shall accrue interest beginning on the [***] following the date on which such payment was due, calculated at the [***] for the due date, or, if lower, the maximum rate permitted by law, calculated from the due date until paid in full. However, no interest shall accrue if the payment is made within 5 business days of the originally scheduled due date. Each payment made after the due date shall be accompanied by all interest so accrued.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the sentence 'However, no interest shall accrue if the payment is made within 5 business days of the originally scheduled due date,' a contradiction is introduced. The original text specifies that interest accrues beginning on the '[***] following the date on which such payment was due'. This new clause creates uncertainty by suggesting a grace period where interest does not accrue, even though the initial statement indicates it should. This contradiction causes in-text contradiction, since there are two different options now being expressed, one which follows the original payment due date, and the newly added clause which expresses a grace period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any payments due under this Agreement shall be due on such date as specified in this Agreement and, in the event such date is not a Business Day, then the next succeeding Business Day. In the event that any payment due under this Agreement is not made when due, the amount due shall accrue interest beginning on the [***] following the date on which such payment was due, calculated at the [***] for the due date, or, if lower, the maximum rate permitted by law, calculated from the due date until paid in full. However, no interest shall accrue if the payment is made within 5 business days of the originally scheduled due date. Each payment made after the due date shall be accompanied by all interest so accrued. Notwithstanding the foregoing, a Party shall have recourse to any other remedy available at law or in equity with respect to any delinquent payment, subject to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 5-day grace period for interest accrual, which directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text that states interest accrues beginning on a specific date following the due date. This inconsistency in when interest accrues creates a strong contradiction, as the contract now provides two conflicting terms for interest calculation. The addition of the grace period in the changed text creates uncertainty and potential disputes, therefore it constitutes an in-text contradiction based on the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Within [***] following Vericel's request, MediWound shall provide technical data and assistance in answering Vericel's questions (a) for regulatory filings and for process changes initiated by MediWound at no cost to Vericel, (b) for process changes initiated by Vericel at the cost of Vericel for the applicable number of hours at a Full Time Equivalent rate described in Schedule 4.5, (c) for new regulatory registrations, which shall be at Vericel's cost, and (d) for periodic regulatory reporting and questions from regulatory authorities, which shall be at the cost of Vericel.
perturbation.changed_text: Within [***] following Vericel's request, MediWound shall provide technical data and assistance in answering Vericel's questions (a) for regulatory filings and for process changes initiated by MediWound at no cost to Vericel, (b) for process changes initiated by Vericel at the cost of Vericel for the applicable number of hours at a Full Time Equivalent rate described in Schedule 4.5, (c) for new regulatory registrations, which shall be at Vericel's cost, (d) for periodic regulatory reporting and questions from regulatory authorities, which shall be at no cost to Vericel, and (e) for any other form of assistance, at the cost of MediWound.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 4.5 states that MediWound shall provide technical data and assistance in answering Vericel's questions (d) for periodic regulatory reporting and questions from regulatory authorities, which shall be at the cost of Vericel. Now in the modified text, it mentions, that it will be at no cost. This contradicts the previous term, leading to in-text contradiction since there are two different clauses being presented on where the cost will lay on this matter. It is further compounded by adding clause (e), that no matter what, all other forms of assistance will be at the cost of MediWound, further compounding the possible confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ongoing Regulatory Assistance. Within [***] following Vericel's request, MediWound shall provide technical data and assistance in answering Vericel's questions (a) for regulatory filings and for process changes initiated by MediWound at no cost to Vericel, (b) for process changes initiated by Vericel at the cost of Vericel for the applicable number of hours at a Full Time Equivalent rate described in Schedule 4.5, (c) for new regulatory registrations, which shall be at Vericel's cost, (d) for periodic regulatory reporting and questions from regulatory authorities, which shall be at no cost to Vericel, and (e) for any other form of assistance, at the cost of MediWound. ARTICLE 5 PRICE AND PAYMENT TERMS
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section (d) to state that 'periodic regulatory reporting and questions from regulatory authorities' shall be at 'no cost to Vericel', contradicting the original text where it was 'at the cost of Vericel'. This directly creates an inconsistency regarding who bears the cost for this specific type of assistance. Even though the changed text and contradicted text are the same, the changes made causes in-text contradiction, and based on the perturbation explanation, this counts as an in-text contradiction.
